title: OPEC+ To Consider Oil Output Hike By 137,000 bpd For April
summary: OPEC+ is likely to consider increasing oil output by 137,000 barrels per day (bpd) for April 2026, ending a three-month pause in hikes as they prepare for peak summer demand and navigate market share strategies, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday ahead of a scheduled cartel meeting on March 1. title: Saudi Shipping Giant Rents Supertankers as Freight Costs Skyrocket
summary: Bahri, the national shipping carrier of the world’s top crude exporter, Saudi Arabia, has provisionally chartered at least five supertankers to ship oil to Asia amid soaring tanker rates not seen in six years. Bahri has hired at least five very large crude carriers (VLCCs), the supertankers capable…

title: EIA Confirms Wild Swing in Crude Oil Inventories
summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States increased by 16 million barrels during the week ending February 20, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease brings commercial stockpiles to 435.8 million barrels according to governmen…

title: Black Sea Bottleneck Stalls Tengiz Oil Comeback
summary: Storms in the Black Sea and alerts of potential drone attacks at a key loading terminal are slowing the ramp up of crude oil production at Kazakhstan’s giant Tengiz oilfield, industry sources told Reuters on Wednesday. The huge field, capable of producing 950,000 barrels per day, was forced to shut…
